query: Pin issue #88 to opus and set its effort to high before we run it.
note: agent-model:* and agent-effort:* are the core label taxonomy this skill owns.

query: Which model and effort tier should the sub-agent for #142 run at? Check what's on the issue.
note: Resolving model/effort from labels (chat > label > default) is this skill's precedence rule.

query: PR #1234 keeps getting its CI cancelled every time dev moves — make it stop rebasing.
note: do-not-rebase breaks the auto-rebase starvation loop on a PR.

query: We just spun up a new repo — set up all the orchestration labels.
note: Bootstrapping the label taxonomy via scripts/bootstrap-labels.sh.

query: Hold issue #33 — humans only, never let an agent touch it.
note: do-not-dispatch operator hold is defined and owned here.

query: I'm about to pick an issue to dispatch — which labels do I have to consult first?
note: Every dispatch/claim decision must consult this taxonomy first — explicit in description.

query: Is there a way to force the cheap model on an issue with a label?
note: Answer lives here: there is deliberately no cheap-tier label — the one-directional family rule.

query: ready-to-dispatch is set on #150 — is that enough to start, or do I check something else?
note: ready-to-dispatch is advisory only; assignee is the authoritative gate — this skill's rule.

query: The label I added with gh issue edit doesn't seem to have stuck even though it exited 0.
note: The silent-rollback hazard and REST-API verify workaround are documented here.

query: This issue carries agent-model:fable but my session has no access to fable. What do I do?
note: Access-gated model fallback: release the claim, leave the label — this skill's procedure.

query: Set the Priority field on PROJ-77 to Highest and add the agent-effort-high label in Jira.
note: Cross-tracker confuser — same control-plane idea, but Jira fields/labels route to jira-issue-fields.

query: Lock issue #205 and post the claim comment before dispatch.
note: Placing the actual claim (label+assignee+comment) is the locking protocol; labels only defines agent-claimed.

query: File this crash as a GitHub issue and apply the right type and priority labels at filing time.
note: Filing-time type/priority taxonomy and the issue body belong to gh-issue-filing.

query: Dispatch #62 on opus at max effort with the full brief.
note: Building/sending the dispatch brief is dispatching-subagents; it consumes the labels but isn't this skill.

query: Run the whole queue — fill the slots and keep N agents going.
note: The slot loop reads labels each round but the ask is the loop itself.

query: PR #501 is BLOCKED and DIRTY — rescue it and get it merging.
note: PR rescue/merge lifecycle; do-not-rebase is one tactic it may use, not the ask.

query: Set up the Jira project's issue types and agent-model labels for the new team.
note: Cross-tracker bootstrap confuser — Jira side is jira-issue-fields.

query: Groom #150 with the lead until the story is clear, then we'll consider it.
note: Interactive grooming for intent, not the label control plane.

query: Print shipping labels for the three packages going out today.
note: Truly unrelated — physical shipping labels, keyword 'labels' only.

query: Recon #210 and tell me the LoE and which model to dispatch it on.
note: Technical-recon decides the dispatch model/effort recommendation; labels is consulted, not the ask.
